title: Blotterbook — improvement backlog (paste-ready prompts)
intro: From the 2026-06-25 audit. Each item is a self-contained prompt: scope, goal, constraints, "done when".
tags: Tags: P1 do-soon · P2 durable win · P3 polish. Send them one at a time (one surface/concern per turn).
note: Committed source of truth for the engineering backlog (replaces the retired .claude/backlog.md). The admin panel shows category/effort/status only; prompt + doneNote stay here.
commitConvention: Conventional-commit titles (enables CH12 automated versioning): when committing, and ESPECIALLY when titling a PR / squash-merge, lead the title with a conventional-commit type so the version bump can be derived automatically — `feat:` (new capability → minor), `fix:` (bug fix → patch), `chore:`/`refactor:`/`docs:`/`style:`/`test:`/`perf:` (→ patch), and append `!` (e.g. `feat!:`) or a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer for a breaking change (→ major). Scope it when useful, e.g. `feat(staging): …`. Untyped/legacy titles default to a patch bump. Keep the existing Co-Authored-By and Claude-Session trailers on the commit body.

items.title: Close the CSV→HTML injection (XSS)
items.prompt: Sanitize the futures symbol so a crafted CSV symbol can't inject HTML. In `app/adapters.js`, `rootSym()` (~line 48) currently lets arbitrary characters through; restrict its output to a safe charset (e.g. `[A-Z0-9._-]`). Verify the raw symbol is never rendered unescaped either — check the interpolation points in `app/app.js` (~493 live cost table, ~1164 exported report written via `w.document.write`, ~1385/1388 data-manager rows, ~1421 trade editor) and apply the existing `esc()` where a raw symbol still reaches `innerHTML`/`document.write`. Don't touch trade math. Done when: importing a CSV whose symbol contains `` renders inert text everywhere (dashboard, data manager, exported report) and adapter tests still pass.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `rootSym()` (`app/adapters.js:52`) now strips to `[A-Z0-9._-]` at the source, and `esc()` was applied at every sink (`app/app.js` cost table ~493, exported report ~1165, data-manager rows ~1385/1388, trade editor ~1421 — incl. `side`). Verified in-browser: `rootSym('MES')` → `MESIMGSRCXONERRORALERT1`; a stored payload renders as escaped text (no injected element, XSS never fired); legit symbols (MES/MNQ/MCL) still render; 55/55 adapter tests pass.

items.title: Add a Pages `_headers` file with CSP + security headers
items.prompt: Add a Cloudflare Pages `_headers` file applying security headers site-wide: a Content-Security-Policy (the app has no runtime deps, so aim for `default-src 'self'`; allow only what the pages actually need — inline styles/SVG, the mailto/Stripe later), plus `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`, and `X-Frame-Options: DENY`. Don't break the existing inline `<style>`/`<script>` blocks or the data-URL screenshots. Done when: response headers carry the CSP + the three hardening headers, and the app, demo, staging, admin, and marketing pages all still render and function (verify in the preview).
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** root `_headers` file applies to `/*`: CSP (`default-src 'self'`; `img-src 'self' data:` for screenshots; `connect-src 'self'`; `object-src 'none'`; `base-uri 'self'`; `form-action 'self'`; `frame-ancestors 'none'`; no `'unsafe-eval'`), plus `X-Frame-Options: DENY`, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`, `Permissions-Policy` (camera/mic/geo/ topics off). `script-src`/`style-src` keep `'unsafe-inline'` (3 first-party inline scripts on home/ admin/changelog + inline `style=""` attrs; no build step for nonces yet — tighten via A1/A2). NOTE: `_headers` only takes effect on Cloudflare Pages, not the local static preview — confirm the response headers on the next deploy. App/demo/staging/home/admin all still render.

items.title: Replace the raw-ADMIN_KEY-to-browser flow with a short-lived signed token
items.prompt: Right now `/api/admin-key` returns the raw `ADMIN_KEY` and the admin page caches it in localStorage, and the staging gate carries the raw key in a cookie. Replace this with a short-lived signed token: the Access-authenticated admin endpoint issues a time-limited HMAC token (signed with a server secret), the admin page stores the token (not the key), and `/api/status`, `/api/config`, and the staging gate verify the token instead of comparing the raw key. Keep raw-key acceptance as a fallback if you want. Done when: the raw `ADMIN_KEY` never reaches the browser, admin writes + staging launch still work behind Access, and tokens expire.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** new `functions/_lib/auth.js` issues/verifies HMAC-SHA256 tokens. `/api/admin-key` now returns a short-lived signed token (TTL `ADMIN_TOKEN_TTL_SEC`, default 2h; signed with `TOKEN_SECRET`→ falls back to `ADMIN_KEY`) instead of the raw key — the raw `ADMIN_KEY` never reaches the browser. The admin page stores/sends the token (x-admin-key header for writes; `bb_staging` cookie for staging launch). `/api/status`, `/api/config`, and the `_middleware` staging gate verify it via `isAdminAuthorized()`; the raw key stays accepted server-side as a fallback (never handed to a browser). Tokens expire. Verified with a Node WebCrypto test (valid/tamper/expiry/fallback). Full E2E needs the deployed Access+KV env.

items.title: Verify the Cloudflare Access JWT signature (defense-in-depth)
items.prompt: `functions/api/admin-key.js` currently trusts the *presence* of the `Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion` header. Validate the JWT's signature against Cloudflare Access's JWKS (team domain + AUD) before returning anything, so the endpoint is safe even if it's ever reached off the Access-protected host. Cache the JWKS. Keep the existing 401 behavior off-Access. Done when: a request with a forged/absent assertion is rejected, a genuine Access request succeeds, and the admin page still auto-fills.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `/api/admin-key` verifies the `Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion` RS256 signature against the team JWKS (`<ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN>/cdn-cgi/access/certs`, cached 1h) plus audience (`ACCESS_AUD`), issuer, and expiry before issuing a token (`verifyAccessJwt` in `functions/_lib/auth.js`). When `ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN`/`ACCESS_AUD` are unset it falls back to the prior header-presence check (route is still behind Access + the middleware gate). Verified with a Node WebCrypto test using a generated RSA keypair + mocked JWKS: a genuine token is accepted; forged-signature, absent, expired, wrong-aud, wrong-iss, and unknown-kid are all rejected. Full E2E needs the deployed Access env.

Done when: oversized images are downscaled or refused with a clear note; existing data is untouched.

items.title: Single-source nav, footer, app-shell, and design tokens via a tiny prebuild
items.prompt: Kill the structural duplication without adding a runtime/bundler. The nav and footer are copy-pasted across 6 HTML pages, the `:root` design tokens are declared in 3 files (`index.html`, `site.css`, `app/app.css`), and `app/staging.html` is a near-verbatim copy of `app/index.html`. Introduce a small Node prebuild script (run by hand and/or as the Pages build command) that injects shared partials (`partials/nav.html`, `partials/footer.html`, the shared app shell) into the HTML, and move the tokens into one `tokens.css` imported everywhere. Output must stay 100% static files served over http(s). Done when: nav/footer/tokens/app-shell each exist in exactly one source, the built pages are byte-equivalent in behavior, and the deploy is still static.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `tokens.css` is the lone source of design tokens — `site.css` + `app/app.css` `@import` it, the homepage links it (3 `:root` blocks → 1; verified `--accent`/bg resolve on info, home, and app pages). `partials/nav.html` + `partials/footer.html` are injected by `scripts/build-includes.mjs` (idempotent; `active=KEY` highlights the current link) into the four info pages via `<!-- include:nav/footer -->` markers. Footers were unified (each had drifted — self-link omission + a howto-only line, which was relocated into howto's body). Homepage + admin keep bespoke nav/footer by design (different links/CTA/scroll-spy), sharing only tokens. Verified in-browser: nav active states, unified footer, correct theme, 0 console errors; build re-run reports 0 changes. **Not done (deferred):** the app *shell* (`app/index.html`↔`staging.html` near-duplicate markup) is still hand-kept — fold into a shared shell partial in a follow-up (pairs naturally with A3).

items.title: Split `app/app.js` into concern-scoped script files
items.prompt: `app/app.js` is ~1,660 lines doing parsing, metrics, rendering, data-manager, filters, journal, and export. Split it into multiple plain `<script>` files loaded in order (e.g. `core.js` = state+metrics, `render.js` = dashboard, `datamanager.js`, `export.js`), no bundler. Preserve load order and globals. Done when: behavior is unchanged across main/demo/staging (verify in preview), each file has a clear single responsibility, and all JS passes `node --check`.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `app.js` → 7 ordered classic scripts: `core.js` (globals/helpers/metrics/format/cost/ refdata), `render.js` (cards/curve/calendar/advanced/break-even + scope/filter driver), `data.js` (import/demo/filters/journal/session/setup), `ui.js` (panels/staging flair/file-dl), `export.js` (report), `datamanager.js` (manage-data modal), `main.js` (event wiring + boot, loaded LAST). Cut at section boundaries in original order, so concatenation == original (no reordering). Verified: all 7 pass `node --check`; main app (initial state, 9 brokers), demo (loaded, curve), and staging (v0.13, 1539-row manage data, terminal, session pill) all boot with 0 console errors; cross-file fns resolve. Old app.js removed; 3 HTML pages load the 7 scripts in order. README updated. Note: staging code still lives in `ui.js` — fully isolating it is A3.

items.doneNote: **Shipped:** all staging flair (activity terminal, session pill, workspace templates, the staging resize redraw) moved from `ui.js` into a new `app/staging.js` that ONLY `staging.html` loads (inserted before `main.js`). `core.js` gained a tiny event bus (`emit`/`onEvent` over `EventTarget`); the `logAction()` calls in shared code (`data.js`, `datamanager.js`, `main.js`) were replaced with `emit('data:imported' | 'note:saved' | 'trade:deleted' | 'backup:created' | 'data:erased')`, and `boot()` now `emit('app:ready')` instead of calling `initStaging()`. `staging.js` subscribes and logs. `seedStagingIfEmpty` stays in `main.js` (boot-time data bootstrap; keeps the boot path robust). items.title: Consolidate ad-hoc breakpoints into a named scale
items.prompt: There are ~15 distinct media breakpoints across `app/app.css`, `index.html`, and `site.css` (760/880/1180/620/1080/780/680/520/460/900/840/720/660/560… plus staging 1100/1480/1700). Define a small named scale (e.g. sm 560 / md 760 / lg 1080 / xl 1480) and migrate the queries to it, keeping the responsive-in-place approach (do NOT split mobile into separate files). Adjust only where a query doesn't already land near a scale value; don't regress current layouts. Done when: queries use the named scale, and main/demo/staging/marketing look the same or better at mobile/tablet/desktop/4K widths (spot-check in preview at a few widths).
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** consolidated the ad-hoc media breakpoints into a documented named scale — xs 460 · sm 560 · cal 620 · md 760 · lg 900, plus the wide min-width tiers 1100/1480/1700/1920 (full table commented at the top of `app/app.css`; one-line pointers in `site.css` + `index.html`). CSS media queries can't read custom properties without a build step, so the scale is a documented convention + snapped literals. Snapped the near-duplicates: `.cards` & `.mcards` 520→560, nav-hamburger 720→760, `.grid3` & `.price-grid` 880→900. `@media` conditions now use only {460,560,620,760,900} + the wide tiers — 520/720/880 no longer appear as breakpoints. Responsive-in-place kept (no separate mobile files). Verified in Chromium at the boundary widths (home 1280/900/560, app 560, howto 760 hamburger) — no regressions.

items.title: Backlog module in the admin panel (migrate backlog.md → committed backlog.json)
items.prompt:
**Migrate the backlog's source of truth from `.claude/backlog.md` to a committed `data/backlog.json`.** This is NOT just adding a JSON alongside the markdown — `backlog.json` REPLACES `backlog.md` and becomes THE backlog we read and update from now on (the old gitignored `.md` is retired once migrated). Yes, it will be committed to the repo; that's intended and accepted, so the deployed admin page can read it.

`backlog.json` must contain ALL information currently in `backlog.md` — including the full per-item **prompt text** and the **"Shipped:" done-notes** — losing nothing in the migration. Suggested shape per item: `{id, title, category, effort, priority, status, completedDate, prompt, doneNote}`.

Then surface it in the admin panel (`admin.html`), behind the existing Cloudflare Access + admin-key protection: render the items grouped by category showing **category + effort + status only** — the `prompt`/`doneNote` text must NOT be shown in the admin UI element (it stays in the JSON data, just hidden from that view). Keep a persistent HISTORY of every completed item (don't drop them), and add a counts module: per category, completed vs remaining, plus an overall total. Done when: `data/backlog.json` is the single committed source (markdown retired) and carries every field incl. prompts; the admin page lists open + completed items by category with effort/status (no prompt text visible); completed history persists; and a per-category completed/remaining tally renders.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** migrated `.claude/backlog.md` → committed `data/backlog.json` (26 items, all fields incl. prompts + doneNotes) via a one-time parser, then retired the `.md` to a redirect stub. Added a read-only **Backlog** section to `admin.html` that fetches the JSON and renders per-category completed/remaining count cards + an overall tally + the grouped item list (id / title / effort / status badge). Verified in-browser: 8 categories, 26 rows, "15 done · 10 remaining · 1 guardrail · 60% complete", and that the prompt/doneNote text is NOT present in the rendered admin DOM. 0 console errors.

items.title: Tighten CSP: drop script-src 'unsafe-inline' (externalize inline scripts)
items.prompt: Remove `'unsafe-inline'` from the CSP `script-src` — it is the biggest residual XSS amplifier (it is what lets an injected script/onerror run; e.g. it is the difference between S7 being exploitable or not). Move the first-party inline `<script>` blocks (homepage `index.html`, `admin.html`, `changelog.html`, and any app page) into external `.js` files, then set `script-src 'self'` in `_headers`. Keep `style-src 'unsafe-inline'` only if inline `style=` attributes remain (or externalize those too). There is no build step, so this is plain file moves. Done when: every page loads its script from an external file, `_headers` `script-src` is `'self'` with no `unsafe-inline`, and app/demo/staging/admin/home still function in preview.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** removed the last inline executable scripts so the CSP forbids inline JS. Moved each page's inline <script> to its own file (index->/assets/home.js, admin->/assets/admin.js, changelog->/assets/changelog.js); rewrote the main/demo new-tab Export report to load external /assets/report.js with per-report data in a non-executable <script type=application/json> block and addEventListener-wired buttons (it inherits the app CSP). _headers script-src is now 'self' (no 'unsafe-inline'); style-src keeps it (inline styles remain). Verified in Chromium UNDER THE EXACT DEPLOYED CSP HEADER: homepage feature explorer, admin backlog, the demo new-tab report (downloads a clean HTML file), and the staging export modal all work with ZERO CSP violations; no inline on*= handlers remain in served HTML.

items.doneNote: **Shipped (defense-in-depth):** added a best-effort KV-backed fixed-window rate limiter (functions/_lib/http.js rateLimited()) keyed by Access email / client IP — /api/status + /api/config at 30/min, /api/admin-key at 20/min, 429 over limit; fails OPEN if STATUS_KV is unbound so it can't lock the admin out. assets/admin.js no longer writes the admin token (or any key) to localStorage — it lives only in the page-session field and autoKey() re-issues a fresh token each load, so an XSS can't lift a persisted credential. items.prompt: Trades are silently misplaced for non-US/non-ET exports. `normTime()` (`app/adapters.js` ~73) always parses `d/d/d` as M/D/Y, and all timestamps are treated as local/ET — so a D/M/Y export shifts trades to the wrong day, week, and month-scope, and the RTH/ETH session classification (`data.js` ~373) is wrong off Eastern time. Either detect the format (e.g. a first field greater than 12 implies D/M/Y) and the export timezone, or at minimum prominently document the US/ET-only assumption in the UI + caveats and guard against obviously-wrong parses. items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `normTime()` now detects D/M/Y when the first field is >12 (e.g. 25/06 → Jun 25), defaulting to US M/D/Y otherwise; a new "US dates & Eastern time assumed" caveat documents the no-timezone-conversion / ET-session assumptions. Verified by adapter tests (M/D/Y stays US, D/M/Y day>12 swaps).

items.title: IndexedDB addTrades transaction auto-commit race on large imports
items.prompt: `Store.addTrades()` (`app/store.js` ~95) issues many interleaved async `get`/`put` requests on a single IndexedDB transaction via `Promise.all`; IndexedDB auto-commits when the microtask queue drains with no pending request, so a large import can throw `TransactionInactiveError` and silently drop trades. Rewrite to be transaction-safe — collect existing ids first (or use a cursor), then `put` the new trades in a tight synchronous loop within a fresh transaction. Done when: importing a large CSV (e.g. the ~1539-row staging set, repeated) reliably adds every new trade with no transaction errors.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `Store.addTrades()` (`app/store.js`) was awaiting a `get()` per trade inside one readwrite transaction, which could auto-commit mid-flight on large imports (TransactionInactiveError). It now reads all ids once into a Set, then issues every `put` in a tight synchronous loop — also dedupes rows repeated within the same batch.

items.title: Adapter parsing robustness (Schwab truncation, blank rows, flip fills)
items.prompt: Three adapter robustness fixes in `app/adapters.js`: (1) the Schwab adapter (~388) does `break` when a row is missing the Side cell, truncating ALL later trades on one blank-optional row — change to skip/`continue` and only stop on a real section boundary; (2) the `parseCSV` blank-row test (~34) keeps a row of all-empty fields as data — test whether ALL fields are empty before keeping; (3) review `pairFills` realized-PnL apportionment (~127) for the position-flip case where a closing fill also opens a new lot — apportion realized by matched-close qty over total-closed qty, not over the full fill qty, so PnL is not under-attributed. Done when: a Schwab file with a blank optional cell keeps later trades, all-empty lines are skipped, a flip fill attributes its full realized PnL, and `test-adapters.cjs` still passes.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** three adapter-robustness fixes in `app/adapters.js`: `parseCSV` drops all-empty lines; the Schwab adapter skips a row with a blank optional Side cell (`continue`) instead of `break`, only stopping at a real column-count boundary; `pairFills` apportions a flip fill's realized PnL over the closed qty (not the full fill qty) so the full realized value lands on the closed contracts. +5 regression tests (60/60 pass).

items.title: Automate two-track versioning from conventional commits + merges
items.prompt:
Replace manual versioning with automation. DESIGN IS DECIDED (analysis done, decisions confirmed) — implement exactly this:

• SOURCE OF TRUTH: one committed `data/versions.json` = {prod, staging} as semver (seed prod "0.12.0", staging "0.22.0"). The platform 'phase' is DERIVED, not stored: prod major 0 → "Beta", ≥1 → stable; platform label = e.g. "Beta 0.12".
• TWO TRACKS: prod (main + demo share it) and staging. (Retires the old per-surface main/demo/staging/platform record.)
• BUMP LEVEL via conventional commits — the squash-merge title's type: feat→minor, fix/chore/refactor/docs/etc→patch, feat!/BREAKING→major; untyped/legacy→patch.
• SURFACE SELECTION via changed paths: any PROD-shipping file changed (shared `app/*.js` except staging.js, `app/app.html`, `app/demo.html`, `app/app.css`, `partials/*`, `assets/*`, `tokens.css`, `data/*` except versions.json+backlog.json) bumps BOTH prod and staging (shared code ships to both); ONLY staging-specific files (`app/staging.js`, `app/staging.html`) bump staging alone; only non-app files (info pages, README, backlog, .github) bump nothing.
• MECHANISM: a CI job on push to main computes the bump from the merge commit + its changed paths, writes data/versions.json, and commits it back to main with [skip ci] (needs `permissions: contents: write`; guard against bumping its own [skip ci] commit).
• DISPLAY = RUNTIME FETCH: a tiny script (e.g. extend assets/util.js or a versions.js loaded on the app pages) fetches `/data/versions.json` on load and sets each `.ver` badge from the page's `data-mode` (staging→staging, app/demo→prod), shown as vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH; keep the baked partial literal as an offline fallback. `app/staging.js` must read the badge AFTER it's populated (await/event).
• ADMIN/API: `/api/config` and the admin panel read versions.json; REMOVE the manual v_main/v_demo/v_staging/v_platform inputs (or make read-only) and retire the KV `versions` record.
• EXCLUDE versions.json from build-manifest.mjs (like backlog.json) so it doesn't thrash cache-bust hashes.
• DO NOT TOUCH separate concerns: store.js DB_VERSION, backup `version`, manifest.json hashes, refDataVersion.

Done when: a merge to main auto-bumps the correct track(s) per the rules, every surface shows the source-of-truth version at runtime (with offline fallback), the manual admin version entry is gone, adapter/auth tests + the build drift gate stay green, and no separate concern regressed.
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** one source of truth data/versions.json ({prod, staging}) replaces the two disconnected sources. A CI job (.github/workflows/version-bump.yml -> scripts/bump-version.mjs) bumps on merge to main — level from the conventional-commit type, track from changed paths (shared code -> both, app/staging.* -> staging, non-app -> none) — and commits versions.json back as chore(release): ... [skip ci]. Badges are runtime-fetched (assets/util.js) with the baked partial literal as offline fallback; /api/config + admin show the values read-only (manual inputs + KV versions record retired); phase derived from prod major (0.x -> Beta). versions.json excluded from the manifest; separate concerns (DB_VERSION, backup version, manifest hashes, refDataVersion) untouched. 24 bump-logic unit tests + 7 Chromium assertions pass; added to CI. README versioning section rewritten. Seed prod 0.12.0 / staging 0.22.0.

howToDrive: One item per prompt; one surface per prompt where possible.
Lead with the *goal*, not the mechanism; let me pick the workable path.
If you batch, name the 1–3 must-haves in case I run low on budget.
Keep using the change-plan + memory pattern for any multi-step item.
